candidates.why: Explicit operating amortisation line above the financial section. In a sports-club income statement, rights amortisation commonly sits alongside core operating cost buckets and appears separate from the selected COGS reference row 'Personnel Expenses', so it may need to be added separately rather than assumed embedded in personnel expense.

Explicit operating depreciation line at the same level as other operating expense rows and separate from 'Personnel Expenses'. However, the label does not clearly indicate whether it belongs to cost of sales versus SG&A/other overhead, so bucket assignment is less certain.
Explicit operating amortisation line above financial items and separate from 'Personnel Expenses', but the label does not clearly tie it to COGS specifically.

variables.notes: Used the existing selected COGS reference bucket exactly as provided: 'Personnel Expenses'. 'Amortization of Rights' appears to be a separate operating D&A line outside that selected row and is the clearest COGS-like D&A item. Generic 'Depreciation' and 'Amortisation of other intangibles' are too ambiguous to assign confidently to COGS without risking misclassification or double counting.

candidates.why: Separate operating depreciation line above financial items, but it is not explicitly labelled as SG&A/admin and could relate to broader operations or cost of sales instead.
Separate operating amortisation line, but no wording ties it specifically to SG&A/admin/R&D.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A-specific D&A row is visible. The available D&A rows are generic or rights-related and cannot be clearly assigned to SG&A relative to the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS reference bucket. Conservative output avoids double counting.
